questions.prompt: Your agent picks the wrong lookup tool ~50% of the time. The model is current-generation Sonnet. What's the right first move?

choices.text: Rewrite tool descriptions. Most wrong-tool bugs are description bugs: vague descriptions, identical-sounding names, missing trigger conditions, missing 'when NOT to use this'. Tighten names and descriptions before reaching for a bigger model — it's faster, cheaper, and usually the actual fix.

questions.explanation: Tool descriptions are how the model reasons about which tool to call. Distinct names + clear triggers + anti-triggers + examples in the description routinely lift selection accuracy from 50% to 95%+ without any model change. Always fix descriptions first; only escalate to bigger models when descriptions are tight and selection is still poor.

questions.prompt: Which is the strongest tool description?

choices.text: `get_user_by_id: Look up a user record by internal user ID (format 'u_<alphanumeric>'). Returns name, email, plan, created_at, last_login. Use when you have a confirmed user ID; for email or name lookups, use search_users instead.`
`get_user: User lookup tool, please use carefully.`

questions.explanation: The strong description has: what (ID lookup), input format with example, return shape, when to use, AND when NOT to use (with a pointer to the alternative). That's the floor for production tool descriptions. Names should disambiguate where descriptions can't.
